markdownRemark.html:
At initialization, if there are no records and autoQuery is false, records are automatically created

Basic use

const ds = new DataSet({
  autoQuery: false,
  autoCreate: true,
  fields: [
    { name: 'name', type: 'string' },
    { name: 'age', type: 'number' },
  ],
});

Considerations

When autoCreate is true

  1. When initializing, if there are no records and autoQuery is false, records are automatically created
  2. After executing the reset method, records are automatically created if there are no records (regardless of the autoQuery value).

Related

DataSet Methods create

name description Property Name Return Value
create(data, index) create a record data - record data object; index<optional,default:dataSet.records.length> - The index where the record is located Record

The create method of DataSet creates a Record record and allows you to specify the corresponding index, which defaults to the last one.

const ds = new DataSet({
  fields: [
    { name: 'name', type: 'string' },
    { name: 'age', type: 'number' },
  ],
});

ds.create({ name: 'jack' }, 0);

Table component add button

const App = () => {
  const ds = new DataSet({
    // ...
  });
  return (
    <Table dataSet={ds} buttons={['add']} />
  );
}

The Table component automatically generates a new button, and the internal implementation logic is:

dataSet.create({}, 0);

Note: The Add button for the Table is disabled in the following cases

  1. When the status of the Table's dataSet is not ready.
  2. When there is no data yet in the parent dataSet of the table cascading.

Form component

  1. The Form component can directly associate with the dataSet, so the Form uses the record record corresponding to dataSet.current. If the dataSet is set to autoCreate to true and autoQuery to false, a new record will be created during initialization and assigned to dataSet.current, and if the corresponding field is set to defaultValue, it will also be assigned directly.
  2. Form can also directly specify record records, if there are multiple records in the dataSet and you want to display a specific one, you can directly set the record without associating the dataSet.

Try it

The following example shows the phenomenon of switching settings to view from associated dataSet, with and without autoCreate
